steps.title: Use the area ratio
steps.content: For similar rectangles, areas scale by the square of the linear scale factor. Compare the two areas first, then take the square root to find the side-length scale factor.

steps.title: Find the linear scale factor from the area ratio
steps.content: For similar figures, if $k$ is the linear scale factor, areas scale by $k^2$. The area ratio is $\frac{90}{810} = \frac{1}{9}$. Therefore $k^2 = \frac{1}{9}$, giving $k = \frac{1}{3}$.

steps.title: Apply the scale factor to the longest side
steps.content: The longest side of rectangle JKLM is $45$ inches. Since all linear dimensions scale by $k = \frac{1}{3}$, the longest side of rectangle PQRS is $45 \times \frac{1}{3} = 15$ inches. C is correct.
